frameworks.name: Bayesian internal model of sensory cortex
frameworks.one_line: Cortex maintains a probabilistic model whose prior is reflected in spontaneous activity.

frameworks.name: natural scene statistics adaptation
frameworks.one_line: Internal model becomes statistically optimal for natural stimulus distributions during development.

core_question: Does spontaneous cortical activity reflect a statistically optimal internal model of natural environmental statistics?
key_assumptions: spontaneous activity encodes prior expectations of an internal model
an optimal model implies prior matches the marginal distribution of inferences
natural scenes approximate the relevant environmental statistics
manipulations: stimulus class (natural scenes vs. other stimuli)
key_findings: similarity between spontaneous and evoked visual cortical activity increased with age
this similarity was specific to activity evoked by natural scenes
developmental convergence held in awake ferret visual cortex
interpretations: spontaneous activity reflects a prior in a Bayesian internal model of the environment
visual cortical internal models progressively adapt to natural-scene statistics during development
